summary: The paper has a credible diagnostic reframing and the headline tables are numerically supported by the current result artifacts, but the quality gate is still blocked by aggregate-only evidence, underspecified signal-audit definitions, and incomplete uncertainty accounting. I used exp/results/final_pareto_synthesis.json as the representative summary because exp/results/summary.md is absent, and cross-checked the manuscript against gsm8k_main_shortlist.json, diag_signal_gap_audit.json, diag_math500_shortlist.json, diag_humaneval_guard_boundary.json, and the earlier pilot summary.

issues.description: The central diagnostic thesis is still supported mainly by single-seed, small-slice aggregates. The proposal explicitly requires benefit buckets as mechanism evidence (idea/proposal.md:113-125), and the paper itself lists benefit buckets plus seed checks as future work rather than delivered evidence (writing/paper.md:142-146). Current headline evidence remains GSM8K n=100 seed=42, MATH500 n=100, and HumanEval n=50 (exp/results/gsm8k_main_shortlist.json:5-15; exp/results/diag_math500_shortlist.json:5-20; exp/results/diag_humaneval_guard_boundary.json:6-27).
issues.suggestion: Add a benefit-bucket audit and at least one seed-sensitivity spot-check for the headline pairwise comparisons, or further soften the mechanism and task-dependence claims.

issues.description: The signal audit is not defined cleanly enough for external review. The paper states that calibration is the strongest observer with d(s)=0.6225 (writing/paper.md:89-99), but the underlying audit defines that number as held-out |entropy_error_corr| rather than as a direct calibration metric such as ECE (exp/results/diag_signal_gap_audit.json:13-18; exp/results/diagnostic_calibration_heldout.json:8-14).
issues.suggestion: Define d(s) and g(s) explicitly in the paper, separate calibration quality from entropy-error correlation, and rename the audit row if needed so reviewers cannot read it as metric conflation.

issues.description: The manuscript does not directly explain the shrink from the earlier stronger pilot revision story to the current diagnostic-only framing. Earlier pilot assets reported CARD-84=0.43 and CARD-68=0.41 on GSM8K, while the final synthesis pivots because TIGER only ties entropy at 0.39 and still trails CORE at 0.46 (exp/history/pilots_pre_tiger_1773250838/pilot_summary.json:72-87; exp/results/final_pareto_synthesis.json:5-18,21-43). Without an explicit shrinkage narrative, reviewers may suspect instability or cherry-picking.
issues.suggestion: Add one frank paragraph or appendix note explaining the pilot-to-shortlist shrinkage and why the paper now rejects a method-forward claim.

issues.description: The honest-compute protocol is promising but still under-justified as a fairness standard. The paper reports actual NFE, latency, batch size, backend, and compile status (writing/paper.md:52-54,72-83), yet it does not explain why actual NFE is the primary normalization axis when systems settings differ sharply across methods, especially CORE batch_size=1 versus revision batch_size=32 and baseline compile differences (exp/results/gsm8k_main_shortlist.json:7-20,21-45).
issues.suggestion: State the normalization philosophy explicitly and add an appendix-grade runtime-lineage/fairness table showing which differences are treated as algorithmic versus implementation-level confounds.

issues.description: The writing pipeline is not fully synchronized. writing/paper.md still contains a Figure 2 TODO placeholder (writing/paper.md:60-64), while writing/latex/main.tex already has a rendered protocol figure (writing/latex/main.tex:75-99).
issues.suggestion: Keep the markdown and LaTeX artifacts aligned so the review trail and final manuscript tell the same story.

risks: Top-tier reviewers may see the paper as strategically correct but empirically under-closed because the mechanism evidence is still aggregate-only.
The task-dependence claim may be judged as over-generalized from boundary slices rather than fully validated across tasks.
The observer-versus-controller contribution may be challenged on definitional grounds if calibration, entropy correlation, and controller gain are not disentangled more carefully.
Reproducibility confidence may drop if runtime-lineage and fairness metadata remain implicit rather than appendix-grade.
If pilot-to-current shrinkage is not disclosed directly, reviewers may suspect narrative selection rather than honest scope control.
evidence_gaps: No benefit-bucket analysis for harmed, fixed, and no-effect revision cases.
No seed-sensitivity or variance estimate for the headline GSM8K comparisons.
No explicit derivation and interpretation section for d(s) and g(s) in the manuscript.
No appendix-grade runtime fairness / asset-lineage table despite the paper's evaluation-first framing.
No direct discussion of pilot-to-current shrinkage from the earlier stronger CARD/TIGER story to the final diagnostic framing.
The generic PPL-diversity tradeoff check is not applicable to the current GSM8K/MATH500/HumanEval suite, and the paper does not make a diversity claim.
